title: Docker Compose Production Guide
description: Master Docker Compose for multi-container applications — from local dev to production-grade deployments with health checks, networking, and secrets.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
Docker Compose transforms complex multi-container setups into a single declarative YAML file. This guide covers everything from basic local development to production-hardened deployments.
Architecture
graph LR
  A[Nginx Proxy] --> B[App Container]
  B
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:r}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(o){const t={a:"a",code:"code",em:"em",h2:"h2",li:"li",p:"p",pre:"pre",table:"table",tbody:"tbody",td:"td",th:"th",thead:"thead",tr:"tr",ul:"ul",...o.components},{Callout:c,Tab:i,Tabs:d}=t;return c||_missingMdxReference("Callout",!0),i||_missingMdxReference("Tab",!0),d||_missingMdxReference("Tabs",!0),r(e,{children:[n(t.h2,{id:"overview",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#overview",children:"Overview"})}),"
",n(t.p,{children:"Docker Compose transforms complex multi-container setups into a single declarative YAML file. This guide covers everything from basic local development to production-hardened deployments."}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"architecture",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#architecture",children:"Architecture"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-mermaid",children:"graph LR
  A[Nginx Proxy] --\x3e B[App Container]
  B --\x3e C[PostgreSQL]
  B --\x3e D[Redis Cache]
  B --\x3e E[S3 / Volume]
  F[Docker Network] -.->|bridge| A
  F -.->|bridge| B
  F -.->|bridge| C
  F -.->|bridge| D
"})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"basic-compose-file",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#basic-compose-file",children:"Basic Compose File"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="docker-compose.yml"',title:"docker-compose.yml",children:'version: '3.9'

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      target: production
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}
    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_started
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - app-network

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - app-network

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    networks:
      - app-network

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro
      - ./certs:/etc/ssl/certs:ro
    depends_on:
      - app
    networks:
      - app-network

volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:

networks:
  app-network:
    driver: bridge
'})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"multi-stage-dockerfile",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#multi-stage-dockerfile",children:"Multi-Stage Dockerfile"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-dockerfile",metastring:'title="Dockerfile"',title:"Dockerfile",children:'# ---- Base ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS base
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./

# ---- Dependencies ----
FROM base AS deps
RUN npm ci --only=production

# ---- Build ----
FROM base AS builder
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# ---- Production ----
FROM node:22-alpine AS production
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production

COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json .

RUN addgroup -g 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser -S nextjs -u 1001 && \
    chown -R nextjs:nodejs /app

USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
'})}),"
",n(c,{type:"note",children:n(t.p,{children:"Multi-stage builds drastically reduce image size. A typical Node.js app goes from ~1.2GB to ~150MB."})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"environment-management",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#environment-management",children:"Environment Management"})}),"
",r(d,{children:[n(i,{title:"Development",children:n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-env",metastring:'title=".env.dev"',title:".env.dev",children:"NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:dev@localhost:5432/mydb
REDIS_PASSWORD=devpass
POSTGRES_DB=mydb
POSTGRES_USER=postgres
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=dev
"})})}),n(i,{title:"Production",children:n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-env",metastring:'title=".env.prod"',title:".env.prod",children:"NODE_ENV=production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://prod_user:${SECRET}@db:5432/proddb
REDIS_PASSWORD=${REDIS_SECRET}
POSTGRES_DB=proddb
POSTGRES_USER=prod_user
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_SECRET}
"})})})]}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"common-commands",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#common-commands",children:"Common Commands"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="compose-commands.sh"',title:"compose-commands.sh",children:"# Start all services in background
docker compose up -d

# Start specific service
docker compose up -d app

# View logs
docker compose logs -f app

# Scale a service
docker compose up -d --scale app=3

# Execute command in container
docker compose exec app sh

# Rebuild without cache
docker compose build --no-cache

# Stop and remove volumes
docker compose down -v

# Pull latest images
docker compose pull
"})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"health-checks",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#health-checks",children:"Health Checks"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="healthcheck.yml"',title:"healthcheck.yml",children:'services:
  app:
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 40s
'})}),"
",n(c,{type:"tip",children:r(t.p,{children:["Always add health checks before ",n(t.code,{children:"depends_on"}),". Without them, Compose only waits for the container to ",n(t.em,{children:"start"}),", not for the service to be ",n(t.em,{children:"ready"}),"."]})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"secrets-management",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#secrets-management",children:"Secrets Management"})}),"
",n(t.pre,{children:n(t.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="docker-compose.secrets.yml"',title:"docker-compose.secrets.yml",children:"services:
  app:
    secrets:
      - db_password
      - api_key

secrets:
  db_password:
    file: ./secrets/db_password.txt
  api_key:
    external: true  # Managed by Docker Swarm / external secret store
"})}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"troubleshooting",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#troubleshooting",children:"Troubleshooting"})}),"
",r(t.table,{children:[n(t.thead,{children:r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.th,{children:"Problem"}),n(t.th,{children:"Command"})]})}),r(t.tbody,{children:[r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.td,{children:"Container keeps restarting"}),n(t.td,{children:n(t.code,{children:"docker compose logs <service>"})})]}),r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.td,{children:"Port already in use"}),n(t.td,{children:n(t.code,{children:"lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill"})})]}),r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.td,{children:"Volume permissions"}),n(t.td,{children:n(t.code,{children:"docker compose exec app chown -R app:app /data"})})]}),r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.td,{children:"Network conflicts"}),n(t.td,{children:n(t.code,{children:"docker network prune"})})]}),r(t.tr,{children:[n(t.td,{children:"Out of disk space"}),n(t.td,{children:n(t.code,{children:"docker system prune -a"})})]})]})]}),"
",n(t.h2,{id:"references",children:n(t.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#references",children:"References"})}),"
",r(t.ul,{children:["
",n(t.li,{children:n(t.a,{href:"https://docs.docker.com/compose/compose-file/",children:"Docker Compose Reference"})}),"
",n(t.li,{children:n(t.a,{href:"https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/dockerfile_best-practices/",children:"Dockerfile Best Practices"})}),"
"]})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:r}=e.components||{};return r?n(r,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};function _missingMdxReference(e,n){throw new Error("Expected "+(n?"component":"object")+" `"+e+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}

title: Kubernetes Deployment Strategies
description: Deep dive into Kubernetes deployment strategies — Rolling Updates, Blue/Green, Canary, and A/B deployments with real-world manifests and rollback procedures.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
Kubernetes provides powerful primitives for zero-downtime deployments. Understanding deployment strategies is essential for maintaining high availability in production.
Strategy Comparison
| Strategy | Downtime | Risk | Rollback Speed | Resource Cost
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:t}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(a){const l={a:"a",code:"code",h2:"h2",h3:"h3",li:"li",p:"p",pre:"pre",table:"table",tbody:"tbody",td:"td",th:"th",thead:"thead",tr:"tr",ul:"ul",...a.components},{Callout:r,Step:i,Steps:c}=l;return r||_missingMdxReference("Callout",!0),i||_missingMdxReference("Step",!0),c||_missingMdxReference("Steps",!0),t(e,{children:[n(l.h2,{id:"overview",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#overview",children:"Overview"})}),"
",n(l.p,{children:"Kubernetes provides powerful primitives for zero-downtime deployments. Understanding deployment strategies is essential for maintaining high availability in production."}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"strategy-comparison",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#strategy-comparison",children:"Strategy Comparison"})}),"
",t(l.table,{children:[n(l.thead,{children:t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.th,{children:"Strategy"}),n(l.th,{children:"Downtime"}),n(l.th,{children:"Risk"}),n(l.th,{children:"Rollback Speed"}),n(l.th,{children:"Resource Cost"})]})}),t(l.tbody,{children:[t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.td,{children:"Recreate"}),n(l.td,{children:"Yes"}),n(l.td,{children:"High"}),n(l.td,{children:"Fast"}),n(l.td,{children:"Low"})]}),t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.td,{children:"Rolling Update"}),n(l.td,{children:"None"}),n(l.td,{children:"Medium"}),n(l.td,{children:"Medium"}),n(l.td,{children:"Low"})]}),t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.td,{children:"Blue/Green"}),n(l.td,{children:"None"}),n(l.td,{children:"Low"}),n(l.td,{children:"Instant"}),n(l.td,{children:"2x"})]}),t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.td,{children:"Canary"}),n(l.td,{children:"None"}),n(l.td,{children:"Very Low"}),n(l.td,{children:"Instant"}),n(l.td,{children:"Medium"})]}),t(l.tr,{children:[n(l.td,{children:"A/B Testing"}),n(l.td,{children:"None"}),n(l.td,{children:"Low"}),n(l.td,{children:"Instant"}),n(l.td,{children:"Medium"})]})]})]}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"rolling-update-default",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#rolling-update-default",children:"Rolling Update (Default)"})}),"
",n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="rolling-deployment.yaml"',title:"rolling-deployment.yaml",children:'apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app
  namespace: production
spec:
  replicas: 6
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 2        # Allow 2 extra pods during update
      maxUnavailable: 1  # At most 1 pod down at a time
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-app
        version: v2
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: web-app
          image: myapp:v2.1.0
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /livez
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "250m"
            limits:
              memory: "256Mi"
              cpu: "500m"
'})}),"
",n(r,{type:"warning",children:t(l.p,{children:["Always define ",n(l.code,{children:"readinessProbe"}),". Without it, Kubernetes sends traffic to pods that aren't ready, causing 5xx errors during rollouts."]})}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"bluegreen-deployment",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#bluegreen-deployment",children:"Blue/Green Deployment"})}),"
",n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-mermaid",children:"graph TD
  LB[Load Balancer / Service] --\x3e|active| GREEN[Green: v2 - Active]
  LB -.->|idle| BLUE[Blue: v1 - Standby]
  style GREEN fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
  style BLUE fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff
"})}),"
",n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="blue-green.yaml"',title:"blue-green.yaml",children:"# Blue deployment (current production)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app-blue
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-app
      slot: blue
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-app
        slot: blue
        version: v1
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: web-app
          image: myapp:v1.0.0

---
# Green deployment (new version)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app-green
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-app
      slot: green
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-app
        slot: green
        version: v2
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: web-app
          image: myapp:v2.0.0

---
# Service — switch between blue/green by changing selector
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: web-app-svc
spec:
  selector:
    app: web-app
    slot: green  # ← Change this to switch traffic
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8080
"})}),"
",n(l.h3,{id:"switching-traffic",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#switching-traffic",children:"Switching Traffic"})}),"
",n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="switch-traffic.sh"',title:"switch-traffic.sh",children:'# Switch to green
kubectl patch service web-app-svc -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"slot":"green"}}}'

# Verify rollout
kubectl get pods -l slot=green

# If issues, instant rollback to blue
kubectl patch service web-app-svc -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"slot":"blue"}}}'
'})}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"canary-deployment",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#canary-deployment",children:"Canary Deployment"})}),"
",t(c,{children:[t(i,{title:"Deploy canary with 10% traffic",children:[n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"# Deploy canary (1 replica = 10% if main has 9)
kubectl apply -f canary-deployment.yaml
"})}),n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title="canary-deployment.yaml"',title:"canary-deployment.yaml",children:"apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app-canary
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web-app
      track: canary
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web-app
        track: canary
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: web-app
          image: myapp:v2.0.0-beta
"})})]}),n(i,{title:"Monitor metrics for 30 minutes",children:n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"# Watch error rate
kubectl top pods -l app=web-app
kubectl logs -l track=canary --tail=100 -f
"})})}),n(i,{title:"Promote to full rollout",children:n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"# If metrics look good, promote
kubectl set image deployment/web-app-main web-app=myapp:v2.0.0-beta
kubectl delete deployment web-app-canary
"})})})]}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"rollback",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#rollback",children:"Rollback"})}),"
",n(l.pre,{children:n(l.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="rollback.sh"',title:"rollback.sh",children:"# Check rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/web-app

# Rollback to previous version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/web-app

# Rollback to specific revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/web-app --to-revision=3

# Check rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/web-app
"})}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"references",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#references",children:"References"})}),"
",t(l.ul,{children:["
",n(l.li,{children:n(l.a,{href:"https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/",children:"Kubernetes Deployments"})}),"
",n(l.li,{children:n(l.a,{href:"https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/kubernetes-basics/update/update-intro/",children:"Zero-Downtime Deployments"})}),"
"]})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:t}=e.components||{};return t?n(t,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};function _missingMdxReference(e,n){throw new Error("Expected "+(n?"component":"object")+" `"+e+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}

title: GitHub Actions Production CI/CD
description: Production-grade CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions for automated linting, testing, and multi-environment deployment to AWS.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
This guide details a production-grade CI/CD workflow using GitHub Actions. It features dependency caching, parallel test execution, and secure OIDC-based deployment to AWS.

Use GitHub OIDC (OpenID Connect) instead of long-lived AWS IAM User Access Ke
content:
const{Fragment:n,jsx:e,jsxs:t}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(i){const r={a:"a",code:"code",h2:"h2",li:"li",ol:"ol",p:"p",pre:"pre",strong:"strong",...i.components},{Callout:o}=r;return o||function(n,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+n+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}("Callout",!0),t(n,{children:[e(r.h2,{id:"overview",children:e(r.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#overview",children:"Overview"})}),"
",e(r.p,{children:"This guide details a production-grade CI/CD workflow using GitHub Actions. It features dependency caching, parallel test execution, and secure OIDC-based deployment to AWS."}),"
",e(o,{type:"tip",children:e(r.p,{children:"Use GitHub OIDC (OpenID Connect) instead of long-lived AWS IAM User Access Keys to authenticate your workflows securely."})}),"
",e(r.h2,{id:"architecture",children:e(r.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#architecture",children:"Architecture"})}),"
",e(r.p,{children:"This pipeline is divided into three main stages: Lint & Test, Build, and Deploy."}),"
",e(r.pre,{children:e(r.code,{className:"language-mermaid",children:"graph TD
  A[Developer Push] --\x3e B[Lint & Type Check]
  A --\x3e C[Unit Tests]
  B --\x3e D[Build Artifact]
  C --\x3e D
  D --\x3e E{Branch?}
  E -- main --\x3e F[Deploy to Staging]
  E -- tag --\x3e G[Deploy to Production]
"})}),"
",e(r.h2,{id:"workflow-configuration",children:e(r.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#workflow-configuration",children:"Workflow Configuration"})}),"
",t(r.p,{children:["Create a file at ",e(r.code,{children:".github/workflows/pipeline.yml"})," with the following configuration."]}),"
",e(r.pre,{children:e(r.code,{className:"language-yaml",metastring:'title=".github/workflows/pipeline.yml"',title:".github/workflows/pipeline.yml",children:"name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
    tags: [ 'v*.*.*' ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

permissions:
  id-token: write # Required for AWS OIDC authentication
  contents: read

jobs:
  validate:
    name: Lint & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-size: 20
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install Dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run Linter
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Run Type Check
        run: npm run type-check

      - name: Run Unit Tests
        run: npm run test -- --coverage

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    needs: validate
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/GitHubActionsStagingRole
          aws-region: us-east-1

      - name: Deploy Staging
        run: |
          echo "Deploying application to Staging environment..."
          # Insert AWS CLI or deployment scripts here
"})}),"
",e(r.h2,{id:"common-commands",children:e(r.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#common-commands",children:"Common Commands"})}),"
",e(r.p,{children:"Below are standard commands to work with GitHub Actions locally or manage runner environments."}),"
",e(r.pre,{children:e(r.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"# Run workflows locally using act
act -j validate

# Install github CLI to check workflow status
gh run list

# View live logs for a running workflow
gh run watch
"})}),"
",e(r.h2,{id:"best-practices",children:e(r.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#best-practices",children:"Best Practices"})}),"
",t(r.ol,{children:["
",t(r.li,{children:[e(r.strong,{children:"Avoid Hardcoded Credentials"}),": Always use repository secrets or secure OIDC federation."]}),"
",t(r.li,{children:[e(r.strong,{children:"Cache Dependencies"}),": Enable package caching to reduce pipeline run time by up to 60%."]}),"
",t(r.li,{children:[e(r.strong,{children:"Use Branch Protection"}),": Ensure all tests pass in pull requests before allowing merge to ",e(r.code,{children:"main"}),"."]}),"
"]})]})}return{default:function(n={}){const{wrapper:t}=n.components||{};return t?e(t,{...n,children:e(_createMdxContent,{...n})}):_createMdxContent(n)}};

title: AWS EC2 Complete Setup Guide
description: A step-by-step guide to launching, configuring, and securing an EC2 instance on AWS — from AMI selection to production hardening.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) is the backbone of AWS compute. This guide walks through everything you need to launch a production-grade EC2 instance — from choosing the right AMI to hardening SSH and attaching IAM roles.
Prerequisites

AWS accoun

const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:r}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(t){const a={a:"a",code:"code",h2:"h2",li:"li",p:"p",pre:"pre",strong:"strong",table:"table",tbody:"tbody",td:"td",th:"th",thead:"thead",tr:"tr",ul:"ul",...t.components},{Callout:i,Step:c,Steps:s}=a;return i||_missingMdxReference("Callout",!0),c||_missingMdxReference("Step",!0),s||_missingMdxReference("Steps",!0),r(e,{children:[n(a.h2,{id:"overview",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#overview",children:"Overview"})}),"
",n(a.p,{children:"Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) is the backbone of AWS compute. This guide walks through everything you need to launch a production-grade EC2 instance — from choosing the right AMI to hardening SSH and attaching IAM roles."}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"prerequisites",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#prerequisites",children:"Prerequisites"})}),"
",r(a.ul,{children:["
",n(a.li,{children:"AWS account with IAM admin access"}),"
",r(a.li,{children:["AWS CLI installed and configured (",n(a.code,{children:"aws configure"}),")"]}),"
",n(a.li,{children:"Basic Linux knowledge"}),"
"]}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"architecture",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#architecture",children:"Architecture"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-mermaid",children:"graph TD
  A[Internet] --\x3e B[Internet Gateway]
  B --\x3e C[Public Subnet]
  C --\x3e D[EC2 Instance]
  D --\x3e E[Private Subnet]
  E --\x3e F[RDS Database]
  D --\x3e G[S3 Bucket]
  H[IAM Role] --\x3e D
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"step-1--create-a-key-pair",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#step-1--create-a-key-pair",children:"Step 1 — Create a Key Pair"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="create-key.sh"',title:"create-key.sh",children:"aws ec2 create-key-pair \
  --key-name my-key \
  --query 'KeyMaterial' \
  --output text > ~/.ssh/my-key.pem

chmod 400 ~/.ssh/my-key.pem
"})}),"
",n(i,{type:"warning",children:r(a.p,{children:["Never commit your ",n(a.code,{children:".pem"})," file to version control. Add it to ",n(a.code,{children:".gitignore"})," immediately."]})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"step-2--create-a-security-group",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#step-2--create-a-security-group",children:"Step 2 — Create a Security Group"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="create-sg.sh"',title:"create-sg.sh",children:'# Create security group
aws ec2 create-security-group \
  --group-name web-sg \
  --description "Web server security group" \
  --vpc-id vpc-xxxxxxxx

# Allow SSH from your IP only
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id sg-xxxxxxxx \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 22 \
  --cidr $(curl -s ifconfig.me)/32

# Allow HTTP and HTTPS
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id sg-xxxxxxxx \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 80 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0

aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id sg-xxxxxxxx \
  --protocol tcp \
  --port 443 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0
'})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"step-3--launch-the-instance",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#step-3--launch-the-instance",children:"Step 3 — Launch the Instance"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="launch-ec2.sh" {5,6}',title:"launch-ec2.sh",children:"aws ec2 run-instances \
  --image-id ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0 \
  --count 1 \
  --instance-type t3.micro \
  --key-name my-key \
  --security-group-ids sg-xxxxxxxx \
  --subnet-id subnet-xxxxxxxx \
  --associate-public-ip-address \
  --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=instance,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=web-server}]'
"})}),"
",n(i,{type:"tip",children:r(a.p,{children:["Use ",n(a.strong,{children:"t3.micro"})," for development. For production, start with ",n(a.strong,{children:"t3.medium"})," and right-size after 2 weeks of CloudWatch data."]})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"step-4--attach-an-iam-role",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#step-4--attach-an-iam-role",children:"Step 4 — Attach an IAM Role"})}),"
",r(s,{children:[n(c,{title:"Create the IAM role",children:n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"aws iam create-role \
  --role-name EC2-S3-ReadRole \
  --assume-role-policy-document file://ec2-trust-policy.json
"})})}),n(c,{title:"Attach the policy",children:n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"aws iam attach-role-policy \
  --role-name EC2-S3-ReadRole \
  --policy-arn arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess
"})})}),n(c,{title:"Create instance profile and attach",children:n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"aws iam create-instance-profile --instance-profile-name EC2-S3-Profile
aws iam add-role-to-instance-profile \
  --instance-profile-name EC2-S3-Profile \
  --role-name EC2-S3-ReadRole

aws ec2 associate-iam-instance-profile \
  --instance-id i-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx \
  --iam-instance-profile Name=EC2-S3-Profile
"})})})]}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"step-5--ssh-and-harden",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#step-5--ssh-and-harden",children:"Step 5 — SSH and Harden"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="connect.sh"',title:"connect.sh",children:"ssh -i ~/.ssh/my-key.pem ec2-user@<PUBLIC_IP>
"})}),"
",n(a.p,{children:"Once connected, apply hardening:"}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="harden.sh"',title:"harden.sh",children:"# Update packages
sudo dnf update -y

# Disable root login
sudo sed -i 's/PermitRootLogin yes/PermitRootLogin no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Disable password auth (key-only)
sudo sed -i 's/#PasswordAuthentication yes/PasswordAuthentication no/' /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Restart SSH
sudo systemctl restart sshd

# Enable firewall
sudo systemctl enable --now firewalld
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=https
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"verification",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#verification",children:"Verification"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"# Check instance status
aws ec2 describe-instance-status --instance-ids i-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# Verify IAM role
aws sts get-caller-identity

# Test S3 access from instance
aws s3 ls
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"troubleshooting",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#troubleshooting",children:"Troubleshooting"})}),"
",r(a.table,{children:[n(a.thead,{children:r(a.tr,{children:[n(a.th,{children:"Issue"}),n(a.th,{children:"Cause"}),n(a.th,{children:"Fix"})]})}),r(a.tbody,{children:[r(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"SSH timeout"}),n(a.td,{children:"Security group missing port 22"}),n(a.td,{children:"Add inbound rule for TCP 22"})]}),r(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"Permission denied"}),n(a.td,{children:"Wrong key or user"}),r(a.td,{children:["Use correct ",n(a.code,{children:".pem"})," and AMI username"]})]}),r(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"No public IP"}),n(a.td,{children:"Subnet not auto-assigning"}),n(a.td,{children:"Enable auto-assign or use Elastic IP"})]}),r(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"IAM denied"}),n(a.td,{children:"Role not attached"}),n(a.td,{children:"Attach instance profile"})]})]})]}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"references",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#references",children:"References"})}),"
",r(a.ul,{children:["
",n(a.li,{children:n(a.a,{href:"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/",children:"AWS EC2 Documentation"})}),"
",n(a.li,{children:n(a.a,{href:"https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/instance-types/",children:"EC2 Instance Types"})}),"
",n(a.li,{children:n(a.a,{href:"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/VPC_SecurityGroups.html",children:"AWS Security Groups"})}),"
"]})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:r}=e.components||{};return r?n(r,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};function _missingMdxReference(e,n){throw new Error("Expected "+(n?"component":"object")+" `"+e+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}

title: Linux Server Hardening Checklist
description: Production-grade Linux server hardening guide — SSH, firewall, kernel parameters, audit logging, fail2ban, and automated security scanning.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
This checklist covers the essential hardening steps for Ubuntu/Debian and RHEL/CentOS servers. Run through each section after initial server provisioning.

Test all hardening steps in a **staging environment** before applying to production. Locking yo
content:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:n,jsxs:s}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(t){const a={a:"a",code:"code",h2:"h2",p:"p",pre:"pre",strong:"strong",table:"table",tbody:"tbody",td:"td",th:"th",thead:"thead",tr:"tr",...t.components},{Callout:i,Tab:r,Tabs:d}=a;return i||_missingMdxReference("Callout",!0),r||_missingMdxReference("Tab",!0),d||_missingMdxReference("Tabs",!0),s(e,{children:[n(a.h2,{id:"overview",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#overview",children:"Overview"})}),"
",n(a.p,{children:"This checklist covers the essential hardening steps for Ubuntu/Debian and RHEL/CentOS servers. Run through each section after initial server provisioning."}),"
",n(i,{type:"warning",children:s(a.p,{children:["Test all hardening steps in a ",n(a.strong,{children:"staging environment"})," before applying to production. Locking yourself out of SSH has no easy fix on a remote server."]})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"1-system-updates",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#1-system-updates",children:"1. System Updates"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="01-updates.sh"',title:"01-updates.sh",children:"# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
sudo apt install -y unattended-upgrades
sudo dpkg-reconfigure --priority=low unattended-upgrades

# RHEL/CentOS
sudo dnf update -y
sudo dnf install -y dnf-automatic
sudo systemctl enable --now dnf-automatic.timer
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"2-user--access-management",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#2-user--access-management",children:"2. User & Access Management"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="02-users.sh"',title:"02-users.sh",children:"# Create a non-root admin user
sudo useradd -m -s /bin/bash -G sudo adminuser
sudo passwd adminuser

# Copy SSH keys
sudo mkdir -p /home/adminuser/.ssh
sudo cp ~/.ssh/authorized_keys /home/adminuser/.ssh/
sudo chown -R adminuser:adminuser /home/adminuser/.ssh
sudo chmod 700 /home/adminuser/.ssh
sudo chmod 600 /home/adminuser/.ssh/authorized_keys
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"3-ssh-hardening",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#3-ssh-hardening",children:"3. SSH Hardening"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="03-sshd-config" {5,8,11,14}',title:"03-sshd-config",children:"# /etc/ssh/sshd_config
Port 2222                     # Change default port
PermitRootLogin no            # Disable root login
PasswordAuthentication no     # Key-only auth
PubkeyAuthentication yes
AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys
MaxAuthTries 3
LoginGraceTime 30
AllowUsers adminuser          # Whitelist users
ClientAliveInterval 300
ClientAliveCountMax 2
X11Forwarding no
AllowTcpForwarding no
Banner /etc/ssh/banner.txt
"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",children:"sudo systemctl restart sshd
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"4-firewall-configuration",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#4-firewall-configuration",children:"4. Firewall Configuration"})}),"
",s(d,{children:[n(r,{title:"UFW (Ubuntu)",children:n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="ufw-setup.sh"',title:"ufw-setup.sh",children:"sudo ufw default deny incoming
sudo ufw default allow outgoing
sudo ufw allow 2222/tcp    # SSH on custom port
sudo ufw allow 80/tcp      # HTTP
sudo ufw allow 443/tcp     # HTTPS
sudo ufw enable
sudo ufw status verbose
"})})}),n(r,{title:"firewalld (RHEL)",children:n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="firewalld-setup.sh"',title:"firewalld-setup.sh",children:"sudo systemctl enable --now firewalld
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-service=ssh
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-port=2222/tcp
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=https
sudo firewall-cmd --reload
sudo firewall-cmd --list-all
"})})})]}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"5-fail2ban",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#5-fail2ban",children:"5. Fail2Ban"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="fail2ban-setup.sh"',title:"fail2ban-setup.sh",children:"sudo apt install -y fail2ban

cat > /etc/fail2ban/jail.local << 'EOF'
[DEFAULT]
bantime  = 3600
findtime = 600
maxretry = 3
backend  = systemd

[sshd]
enabled = true
port    = 2222
logpath = %(sshd_log)s
EOF

sudo systemctl enable --now fail2ban
sudo fail2ban-client status sshd
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"6-kernel-hardening",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#6-kernel-hardening",children:"6. Kernel Hardening"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="06-sysctl.sh"',title:"06-sysctl.sh",children:"cat >> /etc/sysctl.d/99-hardening.conf << 'EOF'
# Disable IP forwarding (unless this is a router)
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0

# Disable ICMP redirects
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0

# Enable SYN flood protection
net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1

# Disable source routing
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0

# Log suspicious packets
net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1

# Randomize memory layout
kernel.randomize_va_space = 2

# Restrict core dumps
fs.suid_dumpable = 0
EOF

sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-hardening.conf
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"7-audit-logging",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#7-audit-logging",children:"7. Audit Logging"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="07-auditd.sh"',title:"07-auditd.sh",children:"sudo apt install -y auditd audispd-plugins

# Monitor sensitive files
sudo auditctl -w /etc/passwd -p wa -k passwd_changes
sudo auditctl -w /etc/sudoers -p wa -k sudoers_changes
sudo auditctl -w /var/log/auth.log -p wa -k auth_log

# Enable and start
sudo systemctl enable --now auditd
"})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"verification-checklist",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#verification-checklist",children:"Verification Checklist"})}),"
",n(a.pre,{children:n(a.code,{className:"language-bash",metastring:'title="verify.sh"',title:"verify.sh",children:'# Check SSH config
sudo sshd -T | grep -E "permitrootlogin|passwordauth|port"

# Check open ports
sudo ss -tulpn

# Check firewall status
sudo ufw status verbose

# Check fail2ban
sudo fail2ban-client status

# Check for SUID/SGID files
find / -perm /4000 -type f 2>/dev/null

# Check listening services
sudo netstat -tulpn | grep LISTEN

# Run Lynis security audit
sudo apt install -y lynis
sudo lynis audit system
'})}),"
",n(a.h2,{id:"security-score-targets",children:n(a.a,{className:"anchor",href:"#security-score-targets",children:"Security Score Targets"})}),"
",s(a.table,{children:[n(a.thead,{children:s(a.tr,{children:[n(a.th,{children:"Check"}),n(a.th,{children:"Tool"}),n(a.th,{children:"Target"})]})}),s(a.tbody,{children:[s(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"CIS Benchmark"}),n(a.td,{children:n(a.code,{children:"lynis"})}),n(a.td,{children:"> 80%"})]}),s(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"Open ports"}),n(a.td,{children:n(a.code,{children:"nmap"})}),n(a.td,{children:"Minimum required"})]}),s(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"Failed logins"}),n(a.td,{children:n(a.code,{children:"fail2ban"})}),n(a.td,{children:"Banning >3 retries"})]}),s(a.tr,{children:[n(a.td,{children:"Kernel CVEs"}),n(a.td,{children:n(a.code,{children:"apt list --upgradable"})}),n(a.td,{children:"0 critical"})]})]})]})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:s}=e.components||{};return s?n(s,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};function _missingMdxReference(e,n){throw new Error("Expected "+(n?"component":"object")+" `"+e+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}

title: Terraform AWS Infrastructure Modules
description: Build reusable, production-grade Terraform modules for AWS — VPC, ECS, RDS, and S3 with remote state, workspace management, and CI/CD integration.
author: Satveek Gupta
excerpt:
Overview
Terraform modules are the building blocks of scalable infrastructure. This guide covers creating reusable modules for the most common AWS infrastructure patterns.
Project Structure
terraform/
├── modules/
│   ├── vpc/
│   │   ├── main.tf
│   │   ├── v
